method_atom: A multiscale (wavelet-style) transform of functions defined on a discrete domain, built from the spectral decomposition of a self-adjoint operator that plays the role of a generalized Fourier basis. A generating kernel evaluated at a scale-times-operator argument defines a family of scaled operators; localizing each scaled operator against a unit-impulse (indicator) function yields the analysis atoms, and an admissibility condition on the kernel makes the transform invertible. Fine-scale localization can be analyzed asymptotically, and the transform is computed cheaply via Chebyshev polynomial approximation of the operator function, avoiding explicit diagonalization.
object_atom: Real-valued functions (signals) sampled on the vertices of an arbitrary finite weighted graph, with the discrete graph Laplacian serving as the underlying operator and its eigenbasis as the graph's spectral (Fourier) domain.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the operator-function/spectral machinery for building an invertible localized multiscale transform with a fast polynomial evaluation; the domain-specific part is the graph, its vertices/edge weights, and the graph Laplacian to which that machinery is applied.
